---
title: "3-assignment-esm-262"
author: "Emma Rasmussen"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Air Quality and For Loops

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Pt. 1 Build the dataset

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}

airquality = data.frame(
  day = 1:365,
  season = ifelse(day <= 90, "winter",
                ifelse(day <= 180, "spring",
                ifelse(day <= 270, "summer", "fall" ))),
  
)
  


# matrix function in R lets you initialize with data
# here we are creating a matrix with nlocation rows and nobs columns, filled with integers between 1 and 10

airquality = matrix(sample())
creatureobs = matrix(sample(1:10, ntime*nlocation, replace=TRUE), nrow=nlocation)
colnames(creatureobs) = c("time1", "time2", "time3", "time4", "time5")
rownames(creatureobs) = c("east", "west", "north")
# check to see that it is a matrix with rows and columns
str(creatureobs)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
